_comment_top: R6 Wave 9 — seed row for the WorkingDocumentHandler 'AppendSection' method. Replaces one of the three legacy hardcoded LLM functions previously registered on WorkingDocumentTools (removed from SprkChatAgentFactory.ResolveTools lines ~811-843 in Stage 4).
_comment_routing: Runtime invocation routes to the C# class via sprk_handlerclass = 'WorkingDocumentHandler'. The handler reads sprk_configuration.method = 'AppendSection' to dispatch to the section-append streaming pipeline.
_comment_method_dispatch: Three rows (WORKING-DOC-EDIT / WORKING-DOC-APPEND-SECTION / WORKING-DOC-WRITE-BACK) share one handler class — same pattern as CodeInterpreterHandler (Wave 8) and KnowledgeRetrievalHandler (Wave 7c). Each row's sprk_configuration.method discriminator selects the internal method.
_comment_security: sprk_requiredcapability = 'write_back' (= PlaybookCapabilities.WriteBack) gates this tool to playbooks whose capability set includes 'write_back'. Standalone chat (no playbook) does NOT include 'write_back', so this handler is unreachable from standalone chat — preserving the pre-Wave-9 security boundary.
_comment_streaming: ADR-033 binding: this method emits document-stream SSE side-channel events (Start → N×Token → End) via ChatInvocationContext.DocumentStreamWriter. The section heading is emitted as token index 0 BEFORE the inner LLM call begins, so the editor renders the heading immediately and the LLM-generated body content streams in below it. The IToolHandler contract is unchanged.
_comment_adr015: ADR-015 data governance: only caller-supplied 'sectionTitle' + 'instruction' are forwarded to the inner LLM call (alongside the optional current document content fetched by IAnalysisOrchestrationService.GetAnalysisAsync as context). The handler does NOT auto-fetch external resources. Telemetry logs the sectionTitle (deterministic structural label) + instruction LENGTH + token COUNTS + operation GUIDs only — never instruction content, never document content, never tokens.
_comment_adr014: ADR-014: streaming tokens are TRANSIENT — never persisted to Redis / Cosmos. The accumulating StringBuilder used to compute the SHA-256 content hash lives only for the handler invocation.
_comment_safety: Spec FR-12 safety constraint: this method does NOT persist anything — it streams a preview the user reviews. Persistence happens via WORKING-DOC-WRITE-BACK after user approval. NEVER writes to SharePoint Embedded.
_comment_description_loadbearing: sprk_description is LOAD-BEARING per NFR-12: the LLM uses the description text to decide WHEN to invoke this tool. The exact phrasing below is preserved verbatim from the legacy hardcoded registration so the LLM's tool-selection behavior is unchanged after migration.
_comment_consumption: Read by scripts/Seed-TypedHandlers.ps1. Idempotent — script queries by sprk_handlerclass + sprk_toolcode + sprk_name 'SYS-%' filter; UPSERTs.
sprk_description: Add a new section to the end of the working document. Use this when the user wants to extend the document with new content (e.g., 'add a risk assessment section', 'append a summary of findings', 'add a recommendations section based on the analysis'). The section heading is emitted first, then the AI-generated body content streams in token-by-token via the document-stream SSE side-channel.
_comment_availableincontexts: 100000001 = Chat only. The legacy WorkingDocumentTools was registered exclusively for chat; document mutation tools must not be invocable from playbook orchestration per spec FR-12.

sprk_jsonschema.title: WorkingDocumentHandler.AppendSection Request
sprk_jsonschema.description: Argument shape for chat-driven invocation of the AppendSection method. The 'sectionTitle' is the heading for the new section (emitted as the first stream token); the 'instruction' describes what content to generate for the section body.

sectionTitle.description: The title / heading for the new section to append (e.g., 'Risk Assessment', 'Summary of Findings', 'Recommendations').

instruction.description: Instructions describing what content to generate for the new section body (e.g., 'summarize the risk factors identified in the analysis', 'list the top 5 recommendations with rationale').

sprk_configuration._comment: method = 'AppendSection' is the dispatch discriminator. This row exposes only the section-append method.
